cases.attack: Treat attention allocation as a sixth primitive
cases.witness: Two agents share commitments and rules but attend to different items
cases.reason: attention priority is recoverable commitment content plus admissibility/resource constraints; deleting those loses permitted-change facts

cases.attack: Treat creative novelty as irreducible generation
cases.witness: instrumented proposal branching and rejection history
cases.reason: novel candidates are commitment contents; generation constraints, grounds, rejection dependencies, and branch history instantiate R1-R5

cases.attack: Treat social authority as a universal sixth primitive
cases.witness: same proposition from differently authorized speakers
cases.reason: authority is domain-specific ground metadata and defeasibility structure, not required in systems without social sources

cases.witness: residual tacit moral salience
cases.reason: the relevant internal distinction is not sufficiently accessible to prove irreducibility or RCCD fit

cases.attack: Pure policy oracle maps observations directly to actions
cases.witness: same action policy under different grounds and revision histories
cases.reason: fails content, dependency, defeater, supersession, and historical recovery under P*

cases.witness: complete finite transition relation with labels removed
cases.reason: faithful recovery requires labels/codebook for commitments, dependency annotations, history and query interface; charged machinery reconstructs R1-R5

cases.witness: states quotient histories with identical future observation distributions
cases.reason: collapses grounds and historical identity that P* requires even when predictions match

cases.witness: input proof to output proof with no persistent state
cases.reason: proof assumptions, admissible transformations, dependencies, proof ancestry and effective queries encode RCCD commitments

cases.witness: merge systems with identical external behavior
cases.reason: P* rejects merging different grounds, defeaters, semantic versions and path-sensitive identities

cases.attack: Replace all RCCD terms in C* with neutral operational language
cases.witness: blind membership rubric using normatively assessable choice, inference, revision, planning, comparison or justification
cases.reason: membership is decidable without mentioning R1-R5 and includes negative controls that fail reasoning criteria

cases.attack: Apply contract ladder from outcome-only to full reasoning faithfulness
cases.witness: weaker contracts yield generic kernels; P* adds independently motivated ordinary reasoning distinctions
cases.reason: RCCD is not forced by every contract, and P* obligations are stated as preservation targets rather than RCCD primitives

cases.attack: Paraphrase P* in legal, causal and epistemic vocabulary
cases.witness: current claims, allowed changes, reasons/counterreasons, temporal provenance and effective inspection
cases.reason: the contract remains stable under independent terminology

cases.witness: proof derivations and causal ground-defeat models
cases.reason: bidirectional reconstruction preserves contents, admissibility, grounds, ancestry and bounded queries when translation machinery is charged

cases.witness: controller state and LTS-PROV realization
cases.reason: predictive-control side is not independently faithful unless augmented with dependency and historical commitments, after which it reconstructs RCCD

cases.witness: institutional legal reasoning
cases.reason: authority hierarchy is domain-specific dependency/admissibility content; universal remainder reconstructs RCCD

cases.witness: two states share transitions but differ in represented proposition
cases.reason: current content cannot be recovered

cases.witness: same commitments with different permitted next revisions
cases.reason: permitted and forbidden change cannot be distinguished

cases.witness: same conclusion supported by different grounds followed by selective defeat
cases.reason: revision response cannot be recovered

cases.witness: same current commitments reached through different supersession paths
cases.reason: path-sensitive identity and later admissibility collapse

cases.witness: case-specific oracle retrieves required facts
cases.reason: without a uniform effective interface the representation falls outside E*; the oracle is charged machinery implementing R5
